solutions.solution: In all other figures, all the arrows inside the figure are directed outside.
All other figures contain different figures with one, two, three and four sides. Figure (b) contains two figures of two sides each.
In all other figures, one dot is outside the figure and one is inside.
In all the other figures, arrows on the circumference of both the circles are in opposite directions.
In all other figures, all the numbers from 1 to 5 are in a serial order.
In figure (b), the arrow of the design (←) is missing which is present in all other figures.
In all other figures, number of line segments in upper and lower figures are the same.
In all other figures, the lines perpendicular to each other do not have any sign in between them.
In all other figures, the wheel has even number of teeth.
All other figures, except figure (c), have even number of leaves.
Only in the figure (d), arcs on one side of the lines have all the curves in the same direction.
In all other figures, both the figures are inverted image of each other.
In all other figures, designs at the two ends of the line segment are in opposite directions.
All other figures are similar in such a way that pressed and inflated portion of each of the figures are identical.
One of the equal portions/sections of the figure is shaded.
One of the two figures is obtained by rotating the other through 180°.
None of the lines on the either side of the vertical line inside the figure coincide with one another.
The figure contains two line segments and one curve.
The figure contains three line segments and two curves.
The difference between number of sides of two figures touching each other is two.
The innermost and middle figures are identical to each other and the outer figure is different.
All the three figures are arranged in increasing order of their number of sides.
The figure is divided into the number of equal divisions which is equivalent to number of the line segments passing through the vertices and coinciding at centre.
The figure contains two identical pairs of pins, each one at different ends and the rest pins are non-identical.
The figure contains two geometrical figures with either two line inside and one outside it or two lines outside and one inside it. This arrangement of lines is same in both the figures.
Four different symbols are arranged in such a way that two are placed at adjacent corners and other two are placed in the middle of two different sides.
Only in figure (b), pipe is present inside the square while it is absent in rest of the figures.
Only in figure (b), placement of symbols (•) and (+) is different from rest of the figures.
In all other figures, all the arrows are arranged in such a way that every second arrow is opposite to the previous one.
In all other figures, the number of shaded blocks inside the square is odd. In figure (d), it is even.
In all the other figures, one shaded semicircle attached to the line and the small line segment attached to the other line, face each other.
In all the other figures, both arrow and pin are perpendicular to each other.

Only in figure (d), the arrow is originating from one of the sides of the triangle and not from any of the vertices. And it is only in (d) that all vertices all away from the point of intersection of the two chords.
In all the other figures, except figure (a), the dot inside the circle is in clockwise direction to the arrow.
In all the other figures, two arcs are positioned inside and three arcs are positioned outside.
In all other figures, the figure attached with the bent line is similar to the figure that lies opposite to it.
In figure (c), circle intersects or covers three sides of the inner figure. In all other figures, it intersects or covers two sides of the other figure.
In all the figures, except (c), there is a triangle and then a black portion and then a portion having lines after that another blank portion and after that a circle.
In all other figures, if the main figure is placed on the base formed by the straight line, the line segment attached to the figure comes on the right hand side.
All other figures are same because if these figures are placed in such a way that double lines become the base of the figures, then all the figures look identical.
In all other figures, all the four figures are arranged in anti-clockwise direction in ascending order of their sides.
In all other figures, the number of line segments at the lower portion of the figure is one less than the number of arcs in the upper portion of the figure.
In all other figures, shaded portion of the central figure and sign (+) are on the same side. In figure (d), these two are in the opposite direction.
In all other figures both the shaded portions have two and four blank portions between them.
In all the other figures, two bent line segments face clockwise direction and single bent line faces anti-clockwise direction. In figure (e), the case is just opposite.

If we denote the positions of the elements as, 1 2 3 4 5, then T oscillates between positions 1 and 2 and N between 4 and 5. Both S and A move diagonally upwards and D moves from position 5 to 3 and from 3 to 1 and then backwards. Only figure (d) does not obey this pattern.
